TextENGNew: I lay spread-eagle, staring up at the ceiling.
It wasn't that I wasn't hungry.
...I didn't live with my parents. I didn't live at the academy.
changeReason: Old says 'school dorm'; new says 'the academy.' JPN 学園 means 'academy/school' (the institution itself), not 'school dorm'

TextENGNew: If I didn't get up and fend for myself, no meal would appear.
I knew that perfectly well, and yet... I couldn't bring myself to move.
It had been several days since I'd sunk into this lifeless existence.
Come to think of it, wasn't it tomorrow?@k...The Watanagashi Festival.
The doorbell chimed.
I ignored it, but the ringing persisted.
Eventually came knocking, along with Kasai's blunt voice.
changeReason: Old says 'rude voice'; new says 'blunt voice.' JPN 無神経 means 'insensitive/tactless/blunt' rather than 'rude,' conveying a different character impression

TextENGNew: ``Shion-san.@k@vS06/15/151500117. If you're in, please open the door.@k@vS06/15/151500118. I've brought you food.``
...I didn't recall asking him to buy me anything.
But he knew I hadn't been eating properly, that I'd lost all energy and spent my days lying around.
``...As I suspected, you haven't been eating.``
``It's fine.@k@vS06/06/150600714. ...I'm not hungry.``
But the moment Kasai set the bento on the table and the aroma reached me, my stomach reacted with mortifying honesty, growling audibly.
``See, your stomach is growling.@k@vS06/15/151500121. ...You should eat.``
My stomach was empty, but I had no appetite.
No -- I had appetite, but lacked the energy to eat. That was more accurate.
Still, it would be rude to waste the food Kasai had taken the trouble to bring.
I picked up the chopsticks solely to save face for him.
``I thought Grandma was keeping tabs on you these days.@k@vS06/06/150600716. Is it really safe for you to be coming here?``

``Oryou-san has been preoccupied with festival preparations and the like. She doesn't seem to have the bandwidth to worry about you at the moment.@k@vS06/15/151500123. ...Don't you like cream croquettes?``
``Mm... they're good.@k@vS06/06/150600718. ...Thanks.``
While I picked at the bento, Kasai rummaged through the magazines I'd left strewn about and read them.
I'd never confided this sort of thing to Kasai before.
...Having always considered myself psychologically superior to him, I hesitated to show him my weakness.
``Struggles like those aren't a bad thing at your age, you know.``
``...!@k@vS06/06/150600720. Kasai, you... you know everything...?!``
``Mion-san told me a little.@k@vS06/15/151500126. She asked me to look out for you.``
``She's been worried about you.@k@vS06/15/151500128. Mion-san has.``
Without responding, I tossed the last cream croquette into my mouth -- the one I'd been saving.
``I assumed you'd want to know how Houjou Satoshi-kun is doing.@k@vS06/15/151500130. I've gathered some information. Or was that presumptuous of me?``
``That was presumptuous.@k@vS06/06/150600724. ...But since you went to the trouble, I suppose I'll hear you out. To honor your efforts, naturally.``
Kasai let out a small laugh, but stopped himself immediately, as though thinking better of it.
``It seems Houjou Teppei has left the house.@k@vS06/15/151500132. Apparently he has a woman he's close with in Okinomiya and has moved in with her.``
``Teppei?@k@vS06/06/150600726. Who?@k@vS06/06/150600727. ...Oh, Satoshi-kun's uncle.``

``That leaves the aunt, Houjou Tamae, along with Satoshi and Satoko -- just the three of them now.@k@vS06/15/151500134. Tamae has apparently realized that Teppei ran off to another woman's place, and her mood has soured considerably.``
``Which means...@k@vS06/06/150600729. the aunt's bullying of Satoko is getting even worse, and Satoshi-kun is catching even more of the fallout.``
``I can't say for certain about Satoshi-kun, but Satoko-san's situation is dire enough that the neighbors are talking about it.``
And whatever bad situation Satoko was in, Satoshi-kun was inevitably dragged into it too.@kSame thing.
``Can't anything be done about that aunt?@k@vS06/06/150600731. She's one of the hated Houjou clan, after all. Hasn't anyone at the main house talked about putting her in her place?``
If the uncle was gone, the aunt might as well vanish too.@kThat would solve everything.
changeReason: Old says 'She's also one of the remaining Houjous... The uncle is gone, so why not the aunt?'; new says 'If the uncle was gone, the aunt might as well vanish too. That would solve everything.' JPN says 万事問題は解決 (all problems would be solved), which old omitted entirely

TextENGNew: ``...The main Sonozaki house considers the matter settled with the accidental deaths of the Houjou couple, and has issued strict orders not to touch anyone Houjou-related again.@k@vS06/15/151500137. The police suspected foul play in the couple's deaths and have been watching the Houjou area closely. Any further involvement would be... unwise.``
``The deaths were attributed to Oyashiro-sama's curse, right?@k@vS06/06/150600733. So if divine punishment has already been handed down, there's no need for human hands to add more -- is that it?``
Kasai answered with an ambiguous smile.
Now that I think about it...
...When you consider it like this, every instance of Oyashiro-sama's curse seems like nothing more than the main house's will being carried out.
Someone notices the grandmother's displeasure and takes initiative.@kOn the night of the Watanagashi Festival.
Yes.@kThe Watanagashi is tomorrow.
...Will Oyashiro-sama's curse strike again this year?
If it does... who will this year's victim be?
Come to think of it, the curse didn't always take just one person.
changeReason: Old says 'there were cases of multiple victims in the past'; new says 'the curse didn't always take just one person.' JPN says 必ず１人が犠牲というわけじゃ (not necessarily one victim), new matches the source framing better

TextENGNew: The first and third years claimed one each, but the second year was a married couple -- two deaths.
changeReason: Old says 'the victims were a couple'; new says 'a married couple -- two deaths,' making the count explicit. JPN ２人死んでる explicitly states two died

TextENGNew: So perhaps this fourth year could claim two as well.
How convenient that would be.
If the aunt and Satoko became the victims, that would be ideal.
If the two people tormenting Satoshi-kun simply disappeared, he'd be freed from all his suffering!
changeReason: Old omits the subject count; new says 'If the two people tormenting Satoshi-kun.' JPN 二人 explicitly means two people, new is more precise

TextENGNew: ``...Kasai.@k@vS06/06/150600735. Has this year's victim of Oyashiro-sama's curse already been decided?``

``Does Grandma make the decision?@k@vS06/06/150600737. ...If so, Sis would already know who tomorrow's victim is.@k@vS06/06/150600738. What about it, Kasai?@k@vS06/06/150600739. Do you know anything?``
``Shion-san... I have been told absolutely nothing regarding that matter.``
``...Well, I suppose that figures.@k@vS06/06/150600741. The main house's highest secrets wouldn't trickle down to someone of your rank.``

``...That is correct.@k@vS06/15/151500141. At least within my sphere of contacts, no such information has come down.@k@vS06/15/151500142. However... as you know, the main house operates through a multi-layered structure. Those at my level are not privy to its inner workings.``
...I considered asking Sis directly.@k...But she wouldn't talk.
She was far more scrupulous about keeping promises than I was.
If the grandmother told her not to breathe a word, she'd keep her mouth sealed with an iron will.
``...Damn.@k@vS06/06/150600743. ...I wish the aunt and Satoko would just vanish at this year's Watanagashi.``
``...Kasai, I just 'expressed a concern,' didn't I?@k@vS06/06/150600745. I'd be ever so grateful if you'd read the room here.``
``Shion-san... surely you jest...``
``Yeah, yeah.@k@vS06/06/150600747. Just saying.``
I said it playfully, but stole another glance at Kasai.
...There was no sign whatsoever that he'd act on my 'concern' the way I'd hoped.
I couldn't go to the village.
So tomorrow's Watanagashi was irrelevant to me, and I'd never planned to attend.
But I was deeply interested in one thing: who would be this year's curse victim.
If I just continued lazing on this sofa, doing nothing, in a matter of hours another curse would be carried out.
I had no idea why it had to happen every year.
But there was little doubt the grandmother was orchestrating it according to some design of hers.
Delivering retribution, one by one, to the village's enemies from the dam war era.
...Thinking about it casually, with most of the plausible enemies already dealt with, the only remaining credible targets were the Houjou uncle and aunt.
Outside, cicada cries saturated the air, only adding to the oppressive lethargy.
Why was it this sweltering and humid when it was still only June?
If Oyashiro-sama truly was the one delivering the curse... then I wanted to pray to Oyashiro-sama.
Please bring divine punishment upon the cruel aunt who torments Satoshi-kun.@kAnd in exchange, I offer Satoko as a sacrifice.
When I opened my eyes, Kasai was already gone.
The remains of my meal had been tidied away, and the room had been straightened up a bit.
Even the socks I'd left wherever they fell had somehow ended up in the laundry basket by the washing machine.
...Having a man clean a girl's room -- even if it was Kasai -- was not something I appreciated...
Well, rather than complain, I should probably be grateful for a loyal retainer's thoughtfulness.
The clock showed some indeterminate hour between noon and evening.
...I really needed to go shopping today.
The refrigerator was completely empty.@k...If I didn't shop today, dinner would truly be nothing but plain rice.
Just then.
...The phone rang.
Since I'd been thinking about nothing but groceries, I assumed it had to be Kasai.
If it was Kasai, that worked out perfectly.
...I could ask him to bring something -- even just another bento.@kThat would save me the trip to the store.@k...No, that kind of laziness wouldn't do.@k...But just for today...
But.
It wasn't Kasai on the line.
``...Sis.@k@vS06/06/150600749. ...Where are you calling from?@k@vS06/06/150600750. Not the main house?``
``Uh, no.@k@vS06/03/150300148. ...I'm calling from a pay phone,@k@vS06/03/150300149. so I'll keep it quick.@k@vS06/03/150300150. ...Satoshi called me.``
``...Satoshi-kun?@k@vS06/06/150600752. Called you?``

``...I don't think it was me he wanted to talk to.@k@vS06/03/150300152. I think it was you.@k@vS06/03/150300153. ...It seemed like he wanted to apologize for what happened in the classroom the other day.@k@vS06/03/150300154. ...He sounded truly sorry.``
``I told Satoshi I was a bit busy right now and would call him right back.@k@vS06/03/150300156. ...So, Shion, please call this number for me...``
I blanked out for a moment, then snapped back and scribbled the phone number in the margin of a magazine's spine.
``...Thanks.@k@vS06/06/150600755. I'll call right away.``

``Please do.@k@vS06/03/150300159. ...Satoshi's been pushed to his absolute limit lately.@k@vS06/03/150300160. ...My voice doesn't seem to reach him anymore, but... maybe yours... maybe it can still get through.``
changeReason: Old says 'Good...'; new says 'Please do.' with much more following text. Old truncated Mion's full speech about Satoshi being pushed to his limit. Significant missing content in old

TextENGNew: Sis could have pretended to be me and handled Satoshi-kun's call herself.
...But she'd deliberately interrupted the call and passed it along to me.
``Take care... Shion.``
Without waiting for Mion to hang up, I set the receiver down.@kAfter the briefest hesitation, I held my breath and dialed the number I'd written in one swift motion.
The ring tone sounded... three times... four.@k...Someone picked up.
``...Houjou residence.``
Satoshi-kun's slightly grown-up voice greeted me.
changeReason: Old says 'He sounded like an adult'; new says 'Satoshi-kun's slightly grown-up voice greeted me.' JPN ちょっぴり大人びた means 'slightly mature/grown-up,' old overstates as fully 'adult'

TextENGNew: There was still a trace of weariness in it, but... it was unmistakably the voice I'd loved.
...And precisely because of that.
...The fear that giving my name would bring back that hostile version of him seized me, and for a while, all I could do was hold my breath, unable to speak...
``Um... it's... Satoshi-kun... right?``
``Oh... sorry about before.@k@vS06/07/151200190. ...Are you free now?``
Satoshi-kun was... still Satoshi-kun.
...The cold, rejecting tone from that nightmarish day was gone.
``...Yeah.@k@vS06/06/150600760. ...I'm perfectly fine.``
Satoshi-kun murmured something like a small nod, then fell quiet.
changeReason: Old says 'After that, he went silent'; new says 'Satoshi-kun murmured something like a small nod, then fell quiet.' JPN 小さく頷くように言う means 'said as if giving a small nod,' old omitted the murmuring action

TextENGNew: ...Somehow, it felt like I was the one who needed to speak first.
...That wasn't fair. He was the one who called...
Just as I was seized by the compulsive feeling that I had to say something first,@k...Satoshi-kun finally spoke.
``...About the other day... I'm sorry.``
``Oh, n-no, I'm... I'm the one who should apologize...!``
``...You don't need to apologize, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200194. ...I was the one out of line.``
If we'd been face to face, Satoshi-kun would surely have been patting my head.@k...How sad that there was a phone receiver between us.
``...I will never forgive the people who drove us to this point.@k@vS06/07/151200196. ...They might be very close to you, Mion.``
``But... it was never you.@k@vS06/07/151200198. ...That's why... I wanted to apologize, at least to you.``
``...No, please don't worry about it.@k@vS06/06/150600763. I was insensitive too -- I didn't even try to understand what you were going through.``
We went back and forth for a while -- each apologizing, each insisting the other needn't.

``...More importantly, Satoshi-kun...@k@vS06/06/150600765. ...Are you okay...?@k@vS06/06/150600766. With everything going on, and working part-time jobs for Satoko's birthday present on top of it all...``
``...I already quit the job.@k@vS06/07/151200200. I managed to save up what I needed.``
``...You did...?@k@vS06/06/150600769. That's good. Then you don't have to push yourself anymore.@k@vS06/06/150600770. ...I'm so relieved...``
Satoshi-kun seemed about to say something in response, but whatever it was, he swallowed it.
``Satoko has it... far worse than I do.``
``...Satoko... things have gotten really bad for her, haven't they...@k@vS06/06/150600773. ...Is she going to be all right...?``
``...Does she look all right to you...?``
Satoshi-kun's voice went dark in an instant.@k...I realized my blunder immediately.
You idiot...!!
I'd finally, finally gotten to talk to Satoshi-kun, and I was the one putting cracks in it...!!
Satoshi-kun didn't respond to my apology.@k...I let my words trail off.
``...She's been hurt so badly that anyone can see it, she's falling apart...@k@vS06/07/151200204. ...And everyone... abandoned her.@k@vS06/07/151200205. They all abandoned Satoko...``
``I-I... we never meant to abandon...``
Even as words to match Satoshi-kun's tone...@khow unbearably hollow they sounded?!
changeReason: Old reduces entire sentence to 'Then, he finally spoke'; new translates the full JPN about being seized by compulsive feeling before Satoshi finally speaks. Old omitted significant emotional context

TextENGNew: Realizing the sinfulness of what I'd so casually let slip,@k...I could only shut my mouth.
Satoshi-kun was desperately fighting to protect Satoko from the aunt's malice.
...And I'd been wishing that Satoko would die in tomorrow's curse.
...I was the one abandoning Satoshi-kun's desperate struggle.
``...Satoko is... on the verge of breaking...@k@vS06/07/151200207. ...So...``
Satoshi-kun said 'so...' and stopped, unable to continue for a while.
It wasn't that he couldn't find the words... it was more like he was carefully choosing them.@kThat was the feeling...
Eventually... he seemed to find the right words.
Or perhaps simply safe enough words, and spoke them.
``...So.@k@vS06/07/151200209. ...I want to let her have fun for at least one night.``
``Tomorrow's the Watanagashi Festival, right?@k@vS06/07/151200211. I want you to take Satoko to the festival.``
``I don't mind, but... why?``
``...Satoko's been through a lot too.@k@vS06/07/151200213. ...Even if it's just one night... if she could spread her wings somewhere away from the aunt... I think it would make her happy.``
It wasn't that I didn't understand why he wanted Satoko taken to the festival.
...What I didn't understand was why he was asking me to do it.
Was there some reason Satoshi-kun couldn't take Satoko to the festival himself?
The nagging feeling that the reason was somehow connected to Satoshi-kun vanishing made me press him further.
``...What do you mean, why...?``
``...Why don't you take her yourself...?``
Satoshi-kun... fell silent.
He wasn't the type who was good at hiding things or telling lies.
...His silence was as good as an admission that there was something he didn't want to say.
``...I-I just have plans. Related to my job.@k@vS06/07/151200218. ...I have something I absolutely have to take care of tomorrow, so I can't make it to the festival.``
I could tell immediately it was a lie made up on the spot.
...But Satoshi-kun was also stubborn.
Even if I pointed out that I saw through him, he wouldn't confess.
``...Hey, Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200220. ...Could you look after Satoko tomorrow night for me...?``
Even at Satoshi-kun's request.@k...I didn't want to babysit Satoko.@k...But the fact that Satoshi-kun was relying on me... also made me happy.

``I know it's absurd to ask you for a favor right after all those terrible things I said... and as an add-on to an apology, no less...@k@vS06/07/151200222. ...But you're the only one I can count on.``
...I wavered over my answer for a while.
But in the end, I had no intention of refusing anything Satoshi-kun asked.
changeReason: Old says 'She's exhausted'; new says 'Satoko's been through a lot too.' JPN いろいろとまいってる means 'going through a lot/worn out,' and new adds Satoko's name per source

TextENGNew: ``...Okay.@k@vS06/06/150600785. ...Fine.@k@vS06/06/150600786. ...But just for tomorrow night. Got it?``
``Ahahaha...@k@vS06/07/151200224. If possible, I'd love it if it wasn't just tomorrow night, but... from now on, forever.``
``What are you saying?@k@vS06/06/150600788. Absolutely not.@k@vS06/06/150600789. Tomorrow only.``
The way Satoshi-kun said it made it sound like he himself might be gone come tomorrow.
changeReason: Old says 'He sounded as if he were going to disappear tomorrow'; new says 'The way Satoshi-kun said it made it sound like he himself might be gone come tomorrow.' New adds emphasis on 'he himself' reflecting JPN 自分は

TextENGNew: ...I didn't like that, so I pushed back with a hint of sulkiness.
After that, for a little while,@kwe let the conversation bloom with trivial things.
Then, just as we were about to hang up, Satoshi-kun suddenly said something else.
``Hey... Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200226. ...I really hate bringing this up, since you'll probably think I've lost my mind, but...``
``...Mion, do you... ahahahahaha.@k@vS06/07/151200228. ...You don't believe in it, right?``
``Believe in what?``
``...Mm... you know,``
``...Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
``Oh... ahahahaha.@k@vS06/06/150600794. What are you talking about, Satoshi-kun...?``
``Oyashiro-sama, you know... if you try to abandon the village and run away, you get cursed, right?``
That was the rule.
Every kid around here knew it.

``...Ahaha, yeah, that's how the story goes.@k@vS06/06/150600796. What brought this on?@k@vS06/06/150600797. Out of nowhere.@k@vS06/06/150600798. ...Don't tell me you're thinking of leaving the village?``
I said it jokingly, but underneath, I was desperate for him to deny it.
``...I'm not thinking about that anymore.@k@vS06/07/151200233. ...Not anymore.``
The way Satoshi-kun said it was an implicit admission that he had once considered it.
...Rena had told me something similar, I recalled.
``...Well, that's fine then.@k@vS06/06/150600800. If you're not thinking about it anymore.``
``...But...@k@vS06/07/151200235. ...I still haven't been forgiven.``
Satoshi-kun kept starting to say something, then swallowing the words.
...His breathing seemed ragged.@k...Could he be feeling unwell?
``...Um... Satoshi-kun?@k@vS06/06/150600803. Are you feeling okay...?``
``...You've been pushing yourself too hard, Satoshi-kun. You must be exhausted from all of it.@k@vS06/06/150600805. You should really get some rest...``
Unlike when the call had started, Satoshi-kun was clearly deteriorating.
I could hear harsh, labored breathing mixing into his words with increasing frequency.
But Satoshi-kun cut me off and repeated his original question.
``...Mion.@k@vS06/07/151200239. Let me ask the first question again.@k@vS06/07/151200240. ...Do you think Oyashiro-sama's curse... actually exists...?``
changeReason: Old says 'I'll ask you one more time'; new says 'Let me ask the first question again.' JPN 最初の質問をもう一度 means 'the first question once more,' not 'one more time' (a new question). Different intent

TextENGNew: ...What answer was Satoshi-kun hoping for?
...Setting aside what I personally believed... what could I say that would put him at ease?
...That was obvious.
``...Ahahahahahaha.@k@vS06/06/150600807. ...There's no such thing as a curse. Of course there isn't.``
I didn't know what was troubling him.
But I thought that by laughing it off, I might lighten the weight on his shoulders, even a little.
But Satoshi-kun fell briefly silent.
...Was my answer a relief? Or a disappointment?@kThat alone, I couldn't tell.
One thing I could tell: I hadn't managed to lighten his burden at all.
``...Sorry for asking something so strange.``
``Oh... uh... s-sorry...``
Somehow feeling that I'd let him down, I blurted out an apology in a rush.
``...Whether it's a curse or some conspiracy by someone in this village.@k@vS06/07/151200244. ...I won't disappear. I won't let anyone make me disappear.``
``Y-You won't disappear, Satoshi-kun...``

``I won't.@k@vS06/07/151200246. ...Not until I buy Satoko that stuffed animal.@k@vS06/07/151200247. I should be getting my last paycheck soon.@k@vS06/07/151200248. Once I do, I'll go buy it right away... and then I can finally rest.``
changeReason: Old says 'that teddy bear'; new says 'that stuffed animal.' JPN ぬいぐるみ means stuffed animal/plushie generically, not specifically a teddy bear. New also adds Satoko's name

TextENGNew: Satoshi-kun gave a light laugh at the end.
He'd probably meant it to ease my anxiety, but the effect on me was the exact opposite.
...All I heard was: once he bought Satoko's stuffed animal, he couldn't guarantee anything beyond that.
changeReason: Old says 'he didn't care what would happen'; new says 'he couldn't guarantee anything beyond that.' JPN 保証もできない means 'can't guarantee,' very different from 'didn't care'

TextENGNew: ``...Oh... my aunt's home. I have to go.``
Even though his aunt was home and he must have wanted to put the phone down as quickly as possible--
...Satoshi-kun paused to take a breath, as if to deliver the single most important thing he had to say.
``Take care of Satoko for me... please.``
I should have nodded immediately, should have said yes at once, just to put his mind at ease.
...But at that moment, I still hadn't forgiven Satoko.@k...And I fell silent.
``...Sorry for the weird request.@k@vS06/07/151200253. ...Bye.``
Satoshi-kun was... clearly disappointed.
What... had I done...?!
I wanted to apologize.@kIf only I could have just said, 'I'm sorry!'
But... it was too late.
The steady beeping of the dial tone... was so, so sad.
